blocks.body: How to use articles: a, an, and the ‚Äî learn when English nouns need an indefinite article, the definite article, or no article at all.

{ "title": "9. Brief synopsis", "items": [ "A - before a consonant SOUND: a book, a university.", "An - before a vowel SOUND: an apple, an hour.", "A/An - for something new, uncertain, professions.", "The ‚Äî for something known, concrete, unique.", "Zero √ò ‚Äî for general concepts, abstractions, plurals.", "Most countries, cities, names ‚Äî without an article.", "Exceptions: the USA, the UK, the Netherlands (plural/union).", "The + adjective = a group of people: the rich, the poor." ] }
